jobs.company_name: Amazon Data Services South Africa (Pty) Ltd
jobs.description:
We are looking for a Data Center Engineering Operations Technician to join our expanding Infrastructure Operations team. A Data Center Engineering Operations Technician role is a hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting and operation responder. If you like to use apply your electrical and mechanical skills to solve problems, we’d like to meet you. Your work will help to maintain one of the world's largest information system infrastructures.

Key job responsibilities
SAFETY
Follow and maintain the highest safety standards and diligently encourage a world-class safety culture. As a facility owner team, ensure remediation of safety risks/issues in conjunction with other teams. Own the safety initiatives and projects to foster strong safety culture.

ENGINEERING & FACILITY OPERATIONS AND MAINTENANCE

Operational experience and familiarity with electrical and mechanical equipment, including Uninterruptable Power Supplies (UPS’s), Switchgear, Circuit Breakers, Automatic Transfer Switches (ATS's), Diesel Generators, Chillers, Heating Ventilation Air Conditioning (HVAC), Exhaust Fans, Variable Frequency Drives (VFD's), and Transformers.
Troubleshoot events within internal Service Level Agreements (SLA’s)
Take daily operational readings of all mechanical and electrical equipment through routine rounds/log-taking (temperatures, voltages, currents, etc.)
Supervise contractors who perform servicing or preventive maintenance
Manual handling and lifting of equipment may be required (weight limits in accordance with health & safety regulations).
Ability to work shifts following established job plans and procedures
- Maintain equipment reliability and availability to support customer operations

Technical Work

- Read and interpret technical prints and drawings
- Use basic understanding of mechanical/electrical/fire systems and associated controls
- Work with operational and administrative dashboards
- Assist in creating job plans and procedures
- Ensure all work meets data center performance metrics

Problem-Solving & Decision-Making

- Apply general technical knowledge to determine appropriate tasks and procedures
- Escalate to experienced peers when situations require deviation from standard procedures
- Assist with mitigating operational risks and excellence issues

Collaboration & Leadership

- Work on one or more process paths within the team
- Serve as primary contact for local partner teams
- Collaborate with peers and escort vendors
- Provide training on day-to-day operations to less-experienced team members
- Influence peers and team members at site and cluster levels


About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why flexible work hours and arrangements are part of our culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

jobs.company_name: Amazon Dev Center India - Hyderabad - A85
jobs.description:
In the Worldwide Returns, ReCommerce & Sustainability (WW RR&S) group at Amazon, we are dedicated to ‘making zero happen’ – zero cost of returns, zero waste, and zero defects – to benefit our customers, company, and environment. We are an agile and inclusive organization that constantly innovates to create long-term value by investing in our people and our planet, not simply focusing on the bottom line.

WW R&R includes business, product, operations, data, and software engineering teams, who together manage the lifecycle of returned and damaged products. In WW R&R, you will partner across these teams to help customers discover great deals on quality used, rentals, and open box items; get the most value out of Amazon’s products; improve the customer returns experience; and reduce defects, waste, and cost in reverse logistics processes. You will be a leader, a builder, and an owner, collaborating cross-functionally with technical, operations, and business teams to design scalable and automated solutions to customer problems.

Amazon is Earth’s most customer-centric company and in WW R&R, the Earth is our customer too. Come join us and innovate with the Amazon Worldwide Returns, ReCommerce & Sustainability team!
We are hiring an experienced Catalog Specialist to help us grow our business in innovative ways. In this role, you will work closely with our product, technology and science teams to support new Machine Learning (ML) models and data science classification algorithm development – all helping to delight our customers through new experiences throughout their Amazon shopping journey.
Need candidates in language proficiency in: Spanish, French, German, Italian.

Key job responsibilities
• Work closely with our product, technology, and science teams to support Machine Learning (ML) models
• Perform data annotation required to train and evaluate ML models effectively
• Support data scientists in the development of classification algorithms
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data annotation tasks align with project objectives and timelines
• Maintain high-quality standards for annotated data to optimize model performance
• Continuously evaluate and improve annotation processes to enhance efficiency and accuracy
• Strong analytical skills and the ability to deep-dive on complex problems
• Ability to manage multiple simultaneous projects requiring frequent communication, organization/time management and problem-solving skills

jobs.description:
We are hiring a BIE to innovate the data practice for company strategic supply chain product, based in Shanghai. This is an critical position to partner with important stakeholders in Amazon organization, working across multiple disciplines including product design, frontend sales, operations and finance to understand foundational insights needs and building a plan to bring this new service to success.

Key job responsibilities
As a Business Intelligence Engineer with our team, you will help identify opportunities and implement solutions to improve the efficiency of supply chain network for the ultra-fast delivery programs. You will be responsible for modeling complex/abstract problems and discovering insights through the use of statistical and visualization techniques. Further, you will develop metrics and dashboards to communicate these insights to the Ops/Sales/Product teams.

The complexity of research and skills for this role is well beyond basic data entry and research. It involves complex analysis and diving deep into key metrics based on requests, building and maintaining multiple databases, knowledge of software applications to create tools, and understanding of multiple programming languages.

The following provides some detail of the job duties and responsibilities.
Use analytical and statistical rigor to solve complex problems and drive business decisions.

Manipulate/mine data from database tables (ex. Redshift, MySQL) using SQL.
Manipulate/mine data from log files by writing scripts (ex. PERL, Python, UNIX)
Develop analytical tools: deep-dive tools, UIs, metrics, and dashboards (Tableau/Quicksight)

Perform deep-dives and root cause analysis to answer business questions
Effectively communicate (English written and verbal) solutions and methodologies to senior leadership

Work with engineering teams to enable the appropriate capture and storage of data.



Key job responsibilities
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Math, Finance, Statistics or related discipline.
• 8+ years of experience in using quantitative analysis.
• Experience with all of the following preferred: Ruby, Perl, Oracle, Redshift, SQL, Python


About the team
• Master's degree in statistics, data science, or an equivalent quantitative field
• Graduate degree in Engineering, Logistics, or another quantitative field preferred.
• Experience with BI analytics/ reporting/visualization tools (preferably Tableau) Familiarity with supply chain management concepts - forecasting, planning, optimization, and logistics - gained through work experience or graduate level education.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills. The role requires effective communication with senior management as well as with colleagues from operations and business background.

jobs.description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
Based in Berlin, Audible is looking for a Campaign Operations Manager (7 months limited contract) who will serve as the planning and operational backbone for large-scale content marketing campaigns of Pottermore productions in the German market. This role will ensure flawless execution across all marketing channels through meticulous coordination, project and process management, and stakeholder communication. They will manage the day-to-day operations of complex 360-degree marketing campaigns, serving as the central coordinator between channel teams, creative teams, agency partners, and external IP stakeholders. Their mission is to ensure all campaign elements are delivered on time, on brief, and on brand through exceptional project management and attention to detail.
They will work closely with Creative, PR, Acquisition, Content Performance, Lifecycle, and Content teams, serving as the operational hub that keeps all workstreams moving efficiently and ensures nothing falls through the cracks.

This position is initially open through a 7-months contract.

As a Campaign Operations Manager, you will...
- Communicate and coordinate various marketing channels including media, lifecycle, acquisition, social media at a global and local level to ensure an integrated marketing approach to the customer
- Create and own approval process within Audible and with external partners for all marketing channels managing revision cycles and ensuring brand compliance on both sides
- Facilitate discussions, remove roadblocks actively between internal teams and partner brand teams
- Manage vetting processes for talent, influencers, experiential elements with external partners, coordinating review cycles and communicating effectively to key stakeholders within Audible
- Own all legal and finance operations for large scale content campaigns, actively tracking spends, contracts and coordinating with key stakeholders within Audible and with external partners
- Set up and manage regular meetings and information flow with external partners and internal leaders to discuss campaign progress, track approvals, and resolve issues
- Own all campaign measurement by being a champion of data and help set up campaign goals basis past experiences; help set up measurement templates and own campaign measurement and reporting
- Identify process improvements and implement efficiencies to reduce operational friction in real time. Serve as the go-to problem solver for operational challenges that arise during campaign execution
- Own all media coordination and ensure timely fulfilment of assets per pre-agreed plans. Own the media planning to execution cycle across all marketing channels end-to-end
- Set up and maintain live project documentation, ensuring all teams have access to current information

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.

jobs.description:
RBS SPEG (Selling Partner Engagement and Growth) team is looking for a Sr Product Manager to drive the product roadmap for its key GenAI offering for VMs/AMs across Amazon Stores. In this role, you will partner with Amazon Retail and 3P teams WW to deeply understand user workflows, identify high-impact opportunity areas, and define the product strategy and roadmap for SAMAI - GenAI Copilot for VMs/AMs.
You will operate as the single-threaded owner for the product, driving end-to-end product development from concept to launch and scale. This includes leading 0→1 initiatives, defining intuitive and scalable user experiences, and building mechanisms to continuously capture and incorporate Voice of Customer (VoC).


Candidate Profile
The role requires strong product ownership in highly ambiguous problem spaces, the ability to navigate complex system and organizational dependencies, and sound judgment in prioritization and trade-offs. You will work closely with engineering and partner teams to deliver scalable, high-quality solutions, while independently driving alignment across stakeholders and influencing senior leadership through clear, data-driven thinking.
You will also be responsible for driving adoption across geographies, defining success metrics, and establishing mechanisms to inspect progress and ensure long-term business impact.

Key skills for the role include understanding of tech, ability to deal with ambiguity, to network effectively and influence partner teams without authority, to overcome unforeseen obstacles, and a passion for developing a deep understanding of the product and ops excellence.

Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities

Develop product roadmap for SAMAI, through user requirements gathering, and converting them into high fidelity product requirements

Define the User Experience, lead design workshops and build mechanisms to capture VoC

Design and lead roadmaps to drive product adoption across all Amazon MPs.

Define the P0 metrics for adoption, and mechanisms for VoC to ensure we partner with our engineering partners to make right trade-offs, and build synergies between tech and product teams.

Be responsible for communicating our innovations to the broader internal & external teams, community.

jobs.description:
At Amazon Advertising, we create and deliver advertising solutions across Amazon’s rapidly developing range of products and services. We believe that Agencies are a key strategic partner and build backwards from their needs and in order to accelerate the next phase of Amazon Advertising’s development, we are committed to transforming the depth, scale and ambition of our partnerships with major media agencies and have created a dedicated team across the world to deliver this vision at a local and regional
level.

This is a fantastic opportunity for a senior sales and business development leader to join the team to drive Amazon Advertising’s strategic agency partnerships. Displaying a consultative style, candidates must be excellent communicators, experienced at operating with senior counterparts with agencies and advertisers, and able to earn trust with senior stakeholders across other Amazon teams. In addition, candidates should be comfortable with complex data sets and have the ability to invent and simplify for
customers.

The candidate will possess a strong, hands-on knowledge of video advertising and screens planning; retail media; programmatic, including data and ad technology. Candidates will be self-motivated, happy to work autonomously and highly goal-oriented in order to contribute to Amazon Advertising’s ambitious growth plans

Key job responsibilities
Develop and execute a customer-led strategy for growth for media agencies at local and regional level and serve as face of Amazon within the agency, providing leadership and support to sales, service, operations and technical specialists.
• Build and manage a detailed agency and client team engagement strategy, including business reviews
• Partner with the sales team to drive sales execution, including providing end-to-end management for key strategic opportunities
• Develop commercial structures to create incremental opportunities for growth across traded and programmatic products
• Lead education and enablement of key agency functions including delivery of agency-wide events
• Identify opportunities for agency collaboration around large-scale creative solutions – including pro-active engagement with creative agencies
• Liaise with global teams to identify transformative opportunities for agency partnerships offered by Amazon’s products

jobs.description:
The On The Road (OTR) Manager is a pivotal role which maximises OTR operations and experience. Excellent at leveraging operational and stakeholder relationships, they are the custodian of on road experience at station level, creating a ‘win-win’ culture between Operations and the DSPs.

Key job responsibilities
•Working with OTR safety and site operations to deliver Regional safety metrics
•Lead OTR performance improvement across the region and provide RLT weekly visibility, including solutions
•Deliver Regional WBR for on-road performance (DSP scorecard / OTR Scorecard)
•Lead improvement activities in key priority areas for the Region
•Create sustainable business practices that create a ‘win-win’ culture between Operations and DSPs
•Ensure a best-in-class dispatch and on road experience at site level
•Be the voice of the DSP within the operational region
•Working with site operations, ACES and CO ensure routing efficiency and best practice is followed to improve CPP
•Owns, delivers and executes the regional capacity plans and solves as required
•Execution of Peak readiness tasks
•Plan Regional network changes (PCD, mech changes, launches)
•Coordinate OTR ACES and OTR Safety projects across the Region
•Dotted-line leadership of the Regional AMDO and SMDO Community
•Partner with Senior Regional Manager as key stakeholder


A day in the life
The OTR manager defines the medium-term OTR strategy, leading and influencing both internal and external stakeholders. They partner with Senior Regional Managers, supporting the execution of OTR Safety, Capacity, Quality and Experience within that Area. They further partner with Delivery Partner Managers, OTR ACES and Community teams to implement Network wide best practice. The role is field based with regular travel required across up to 6 Last Mile Delivery Stations plus associated nodes.

About the team
This is an exciting and unique opportunity to join the OTR team in Region 2, as part of the wider UK OTR organisation. This is a highly autonomous area in which an individual with a passion for on road operations can thrive.

jobs.description:
Amazon Ads is the digital advertising arm of Amazon and one of our fastest growing businesses. We operate at the intersection of commerce, entertainment and advertising, offering unique advertising products, enterprise ad tech and a rich array of unique creative, format and measurement solutions. We offer advertisers premium brand placements on our owned and operated properties, including Prime Video ads, Twitch, and a rich slate of retail media solutions.

As an Insights and Measurement Consultant, you will work as an individual contributor to accelerate our advertising business growth in the Benelux and Nordics regions. With Prime Video's launch in Netherlands and Sweden, you'll have a pivotal role in shaping the streaming TV and video landscape in the region. You will serve as a strategic advisor, implementing analytics solutions to drive business transformation. You will lead Ad measurement discussions, create actionable insights, and present clear recommendations to executive stakeholders.

Key job responsibilities
• Leverage Amazon's proprietary data, third-party research, and ad measurement solutions to generate actionable insights
• Own senior-level measurement and insights customer relationships, partnering across strategic learning objectives that influence business decision-making
• Collaborate cross-functionally to influence sales and marketing initiatives across multiple business functions and geographies
• Advocate for data-driven decision-making and guide advertisers to implement recommended solutions


A day in the life
On a daily basis, the Insights and Measurement Lead performs hands-on data extraction and analysis using SQL and Python, leveraging Amazon measurement solutions to build insights and track success metrics for advertisers' businesses and industries. The role requires developing data-driven recommendations for executive client leadership and communicating those insights in a clear and compelling manner.


About the team
The Analytics & Insights (A&I) organization leverages Amazon's data to develop actionable insights for advertisers, enhancing marketers' decision-making and measurably improving their success both on and off Amazon.

jobs.description:
The Site WHS officer will be responsible for partnering with a site operations team in the Sort center to execute Amazon safety policies and ensure compliance to all applicable local and regional regulations. By leveraging lean principles and kaizens, this individual will lead continuous improvement initiatives to reduce conditional and ergonomic risk in our processes to ensure a safe and healthful working environment for our Associates. The Site WHS officer must demonstrate the ability to build trust and confidence with the Operations Team and influence change through providing comprehensive risk assessments and safety data analysis. The Site WHS officer must be an effective communicator and send clear, concise and consistent messages, both verbally and in writing. This individual must instruct and train Operations Leaders in company safety policies and assist the operations site teams in incorporating our safety standards at their site. The Site WHS officer will be required to identify best practices and incorporate these best practices into our standards to continuously improve company safety policies. The Site WHS officer will be required to possess excellent safety program and relevant safety, environmental and ergonomic knowledge and demonstrates this expertise when working with Operations. This individual must create and execute leadership development plans for their Safety Associates on their team. They must communicate safety team expectations and give frequent and appropriate feedback to their safety teams and ensure they are executing to the core competencies of an Amazon safety professional.

Key job responsibilities
As WHS Officer, You will support an assigned site(s), coordinate and assist in the implementation of Amazon WHS procedures and programs as directed by WHS leadership, in alignment with policy and site goals. You will partner with the site stakeholders to drive compliance with relevant Amazon WHS standards, procedures and applicable local laws. You will play vital role in coaching associates on new and existing WHS requirements, including their impact on the site. You will partner with site operations and WHS leadership on tackling day to day risks, identify and escalate compliance risks and improvement opportunities through the completion of assigned routine inspections and audits. You will create actions where findings identified as a result of implementing this standard work. You will act as the proxy1 for the WHS Manager when assigned. You will fulfill the assigned tasks within this standard work as allocated by the WHS Manager, including additional tasks as stretch assignment. You must adhere to the scheduled shift in order to support site safety.
jobs.description_short: The Site WHS officer will be responsible for partnering with a site operations team in the Sort center to execute Amazon safety policies and ensure compliance to all applicable local and regional regulations.
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Master's degree in Health and Safety or a related field
- 2+ years of supply chain experience
- - Experience in stakeholder management, dealing with multiple stakeholders at varied levels of the organization

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. jobs.title: WHS Officer

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- WHS Experience/or WHS qualifications
- Experience using Microsoft Word and Excel in a professional capacity
- Bachelor’s degree.
- NEBOSH or IOSH certified.
- Good communicator - English & Arabic
- Available to work flexible shifts including days, nights and/or weekends
jobs.description:
The Site WHS Officer will be responsible for partnering with a site operations team in a delivery station to execute company safety policies and ensure compliance to all applicable local and regional regulations. By leveraging lean principles and kaizens, you will lead continuous improvement initiatives to reduce conditional and ergonomic risk in our processes to ensure a safe and healthful working environment for our Associates. A successful Site WHS Officer will demonstrate the ability to build trust and confidence with the Operations Team and inspire change through providing comprehensive risk assessments and safety data analysis.
The Site WHS Officer must be an effective communicator and send clear, concise and consistent messages, both verbally and in writing. You will instruct and train Operations Leaders in Amazon WHS policies and assist the operations site teams in incorporating our safety standards at their site. You will work to identify best practices and incorporate these standard methodologies into our standards to continuously improve the safety landscape.
Site WHS officer will possess excellent safety program knowledge as well as environmental and ergonomic knowledge and be able demonstrate this expertise when working with Operations.
The Site WHS officer will be a part of site WHS team and reports to site WHS manager. The WHS officer should be flexible to work in shifts.
Possess a thorough understanding of local/regional regulations and company policy.


Key job responsibilities
- Provide guidance and oversight to ensure compliance to all applicable Amazon WHS standards/Policies.
- Measure site performance against published requirements in safety policies. For all deficiencies, identify a plan for the site to implement and fix all identified deficiencies.
- Deliver on-time and quality projects to Operations.
- Analyze safety metrics and review incident weekly and monthly incident trends to discover trends to rationalize the allocation of appropriate resources to areas where the safety risk is highest.
- Ensure proper Incident Investigation and closure of Corrective action and preventive action.
- Perform frequent site safety audits to identify all non-compliance equipment and/or processes at the site.
- Implement solutions to eliminate exposure to these risks and prevent injury.
- Audit record-keeping practices and ensure Austin entries to be aligned with global WHS standards and local regulations.
- Audit the workplace organization and Associate behaviors to ensure that training, auditing and scoring methods are accurate and consistent.
- Lead and mentor Safety Associates in your assigned facility during peak.
- Spend time at the delivery station and seek input from Safety teams, Associates and leaders on safety program improvement opportunities. Ensure open communication on status of progress and resolution against these suggestions.
- Ensure site leadership and Associates are trained and knowledgeable of their responsibilities under each policy. Audit the quality, delivery (including qualifications of the trainer), and effectiveness of all required safety training.
- Ensure the PPE’s inventory at site and coordinate with procurement will place order accordingly.
- Ensure site PPE compliance.
- You have to implement the best practices at site.
- Implement PIT (powered industrial truck) safety and yard safety system.
- Ensure standard Work Permit System usage at site.
- Implement and ensure safe contractor management at site.
- Ensure basic safety training calendar for associates and imparting trainings as per the same.
- Work along with facility team and ensure preventive maintenance of all Fire protection and emergency equipment’s
- Ensure basic Fire Fighting training and first aid training requirements.
jobs.description_short: The Site WHS Officer will be responsible for partnering with a site operations team in a delivery station to execute company safety policies and ensure compliance to all applicable local and regional regulations.
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- 1-3 years of growing responsibilities in safety and/or environmental programs in manufacturing, production, or service operations.
- Ability to analyze accident data, new processes, and machinery for potential safety concerns and conduct job hazard and job safety analyses
- Experienced in emergency response and currently holds First aid and CPR certificates.
- Experience implementing lean principles and process improvement in an operational environment.
- Ability to adapt well to fast-paced environments with changing circumstances, direction, and strategy.
